text.title: Agent Eval Harness Bootstrap Recipe Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of agent-eval-harness-bootstrap-recipe enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Harness scores each trajectory on a 1-5 rubric with ≥2 criteria; criteria have written 1-5 anchors before any scoring.
rule.rationale: Free-form judge prompts produce non-comparable scores; anchored rubrics give comparability across runs.

rule.statement: Every trajectory has an OTel trace; the harness records `eval_run_id` + `trajectory_id` + `score` per criterion.
rule.rationale: Without OTel correlation the harness cannot distinguish a regression caused by retrieval vs by the model.

rule.statement: Record-replay: every failed trajectory captures the full input/output to a deterministic replay file; replay is the first diagnostic step.
rule.rationale: Without replay, debugging a failed trajectory takes hours; replay turns it into minutes.

rule.statement: Harness fails the CI gate when pass-rate drops ≥5% vs the last green run on any tagged subset.
rule.rationale: Subset alerts catch capability regressions that average pass-rate hides.

rule.statement: Each criterion gets ≥2 raters when the eval gates a >$10k or strategic decision; single-rater scoring is allowed only for pre-PMF speed.
rule.rationale: Inter-rater agreement is the only check on judge bias.
